india.title: Indonesian proverb of the day: 'An elephant at the edge of the eye is not seen, but an ant across the ocean is seen'
india.summary: This ancient Indonesian proverb, 'An elephant at the edge of one’s own eye goes unnoticed, while an ant across the ocean is clearly seen,' captures the essence of our inclination to criticize minor issues in others while overlooking our own significant shortcomings. With deep roots in oral tradition, it aligns with contemporary psychology regarding cognitive bias.

india.title: Mongolian proverb of the day: 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule'
india.summary: In Mongolia, a saying goes, 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule.' This wisdom reflects a strong cultural belief in self-sufficiency over ease. Born from the expansive steppes and their nomadic lifestyle, the phrase underscores the importance of personal freedom and decision-making, even if those choices come with challenges. It’s a timeless reminder that true integrity stems from self-determination.

Analysts attribute this slump to signals from the Federal Reserve and a notable downturn in technology stocks. Investors are now closely monitoring upcoming US inflation figures to gauge the next steps in the market.

business.title: Sebi introduces lighter certification framework for non-core staff in investment advisory roles
business.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has launched a new certification, 'NISM Series-XXV-B', specifically designed for investment advisory personnel focused on sales and client interaction. This initiative is intended to simplify operational processes for financial firms while maintaining compliance standards. However, professionals in primary advisory roles will still need to obtain 'NISM Series-X-A' and 'X-B' certifications, ensuring a comprehensive regulatory framework.
